title: Statutory Declaration for Extended Family Members Outside Canada of a Canadian Victim of an Air Disaster that may apply for a Permanent Residence Visa (IMM 0171) - Canada.ca
content:
## Statutory Declaration for Extended Family Members Outside Canada of a Canadian Victim of an Air Disaster that may apply for a Permanent Residence Visa (IMM 0171)
Statutory Declaration for Extended Family Members Outside Canada of a Canadian
                    Victim of an Air Disaster that may apply for a Permanent Residence Visa [IMM
                        0171] (PDF, 0.3 MB)

### How to download, save and open this form
- Use your computer. The form may not open on tablets or mobile phones .
- Save the form on your computer in a place you can remember. For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form. If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form.
- If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- After you download the form, open it using Adobe Acrobat Reader.

#### Open this form in Acrobat Reader
- Open Acrobat Reader. You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms. Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms.
- Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- Select “ File ” from the top menu.
- Find the location where you saved the form, click on the file, and click “ Open. ”
Read the step by step instructions on how to complete the form.

### Statutory Declaration [IMM 0171]
This form must be completed by:
- the spouse or common-law partner of the victim of the air disasters, or
- if the victim does not have a surviving spouse or common-law partner, or if they are not in
                            Canada; the child (18 years of age or older), or; the guardian of a child (under 18 years of age)
- the child (18 years of age or older), or;
- the guardian of a child (under 18 years of age)
Note : The completed and signed statutory
                            declaration must be provided by the victim’s spouse or common-law partner in Canada, or if
                            the victim does not have a surviving spouse or common-law partner, or if they are not in
                            Canada, by the oldest child (18 years of age or over), or the guardian of the child (under
                            18 years of age). Surviving family member in Canada may sign a statutory declaration for up to two principal applicants.
- Enter your full family name (surname or last name) exactly as it is shown
                                on your passport, travel or identity document.
- Enter your given name(s) (first, second or more) exactly as it is shown on your passport,
                                travel or identity document. Do not use initials.
- Enter your date of birth If you do not know your complete date of birth, please use an “*”
                                (asterisk) to fill in the spaces for the unknown year, month or day. For example, if the day
                                and month of your date of birth is unknown you could enter 1964/*/*.
- Enter your country of citizenship
- Enter your residential address
- Enter your e-mail address
- Enter your phone number
- Enter the extended family member’s full family name (surname or last name) exactly as it is
                                shown on their passport, travel or identity document.
- Enter the extended family member’s given name(s) (first, second or more) exactly as it is
                                shown on your passport, travel or identity document. Do not use initials.
- Enter their UCI, if applicable
- Enter their Place of Birth
- Enter their country of citizenship
- Enter their date of birth. If you do not know their complete date of birth, please use an
                                “*” (asterisk) to fill in the spaces for the unknown year, month or day. For example, if the
                                day and month of their date of birth is unknown you could enter 1964/*/*.
- Enter their passport/travel document number
- Enter your family and given name confirming that you have read and understood the content of
                                the form
- Enter the given and family name of your extended family member
- Select your family relationships
- Type your family and given name
- Enter your signature and the date you sign the form
- Should be completed and signed by the notary, lawyer, commissioner of oaths or justice of
                                the peace in Canada.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
